reasons: Mathilda gives Edith a safer entry or escape route, helping Edith manage the timing between frontline control and Primal Wrath.
Mathilda's shield helps Edith survive the first commit before Edith turns the fight with transformation damage.

proof.summary: Mathilda gives Edith shield and mobility support so Edith can enter, reposition, or reset before using her transformation window.
worksBestWhen: Edith plays as an EXP/frontline hero.
Mathilda saves Guiding Wind for Edith's real commit or escape window.
Edith has Primal Wrath or key control cooldowns ready.
failureCases: Edith enters before Mathilda is in range.
Edith transforms after enemies already disengage.
The dash path pulls Edith into a bad angle.

reasons: Angela can attach to Edith and add shielding while Edith commits as an EXP/frontline threat.
Edith can use the extra protection to survive the first burst window and continue into Primal Wrath damage.

proof.summary: Angela shields and attaches to Edith, helping Edith survive the frontline commit and continue into Primal Wrath damage.
worksBestWhen: Edith commits from EXP/frontline while Angela supports from roam.
Angela attaches before Edith takes lethal burst.
Edith has Primal Wrath or key control cooldowns ready.
failureCases: Edith is suppressed or chain-bursted before Angela attaches.
Edith dives too far ahead and cannot convert the shield into follow-up damage.

reasons: Floryn can support Edith globally while Edith plays from EXP or frontline positions away from the roamer.
Floryn's healing helps Edith survive after absorbing damage so she can continue into Primal Wrath pressure.

proof.summary: Floryn gives Edith global healing support, helping Edith survive the first damage wave and continue fighting into Primal Wrath.
worksBestWhen: Edith commits while Bloom is available.
Floryn saves Bloom until Edith has taken meaningful damage.
Edith has team follow-up after surviving the first burst window.
failureCases: Edith is killed before Bloom matters.
Floryn uses Bloom before Edith's real commit.

reasons: Estes can keep Edith alive in longer front-to-back fights where Edith needs time to control enemies and transform.
Edith gives Estes a durable frontline target who can stay near the team and keep enemies occupied.

proof.summary: Estes extends Edith's frontline uptime so Edith can keep controlling space and reach her Primal Wrath value.
worksBestWhen: Edith fights near the team rather than diving too far alone.
Estes saves his strongest healing window for the main fight.
The team has damage follow-up after Edith sustains the frontline.
failureCases: Edith is isolated outside Estes's healing area.
Edith and Estes sustain the fight but the team lacks damage follow-up.

reasons: Tigreal can group enemies first, giving Edith a clean follow-up window for Earth Shatter or Primal Wrath.
Edith benefits from grouped enemies because her control and transformed ranged damage are easier to apply.

proof.summary: Tigreal pulls and stuns enemies, while Edith follows with additional control or transformed damage.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal catches multiple enemies or a priority carry.
Edith is close enough to follow immediately.
Edith has enough Wrath or cooldowns ready after Tigreal's engage.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far away from Edith.
The team lacks damage after a double-frontline commit.
Tigreal's channel is interrupted before the setup completes.
